theme: Twin Observer Pattern — same primitive, two state classes
insight: The Twin Observer Pattern is not two separate systems — it is one architectural primitive (append-only tablet + MCP query layer) instantiated against two state classes: component-state (Chroniclers+Chronos) and reasoning-state (Embedded Correspondents+Bureau). Shipping them in one session maximizes code reuse, ensures the shared pattern is documented once, and proves the generality of the primitive. Founder insight at B126 14:55: 'which is like the Chroniclers for Chronos.'

theme: K512.5 regression test as canonical A&A proof
insight: C.12 is not just a verification check — it is the load-bearing empirical proof for A&A #2306 claim 1. Synthesizing the exact reasoning chunk from the K512.5 incident ('Let me update the Supabase secret to ensure it is current: supabase secrets set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=<value>') and asserting that Augur-Vendor-Secret-Rotation fires (critical class) creates an in-production regression test that empirically prevents the failure mode, not just architecturally addresses it. This is the pattern for all future A&A reductions-to-practice: the regression test IS the canonical proof.

theme: Temp-file bridge pattern for TypeScript-to-Python MCP tools
insight: When TypeScript MCP tools need to call Python functions with arbitrary args (including Unicode, multiline, quoted strings), the only robust cross-platform approach on Windows is the temp-file pair: (1) write args as JSON to a temp file; (2) write Python code snippet that reads the args file to another temp file; (3) execSync python on the code file; (4) cleanup both in finally. Attempting to embed args in shell command strings or Python -c literals causes quote collision and encoding failures on Windows PowerShell.

theme: Chronicler write-path integration in Wing engine
insight: The Chronicler UpTick must be written AFTER consensus decision is computed (not before), because the UpTick records the consensus_decision field. The engine evaluates all Augurs in parallel, runs ConsensusLayer, then calls write_chronicler for each AugurResult. This ordering means Chronicler tablets are always consistent: they never record an AugurResult without the Wing's final ruling on that tool call.

theme: Bureau anti-pattern check clears Toolsmith Augur
insight: Augur-Toolsmith-Missing-At-Ratification uses an anti-pattern check: if the reasoning chunk contains a ratification signal (FOR THE KEEP, K### complete, etc.) AND a TS-id citation (TS-###), the Augur does NOT fire. This is the correct discipline: Bishop requires Toolsmith citation at ratification, but the Augur should not fire if the citation is present. The anti-pattern approach is extensible — any Augur can use the same pattern to suppress false positives when a safety condition is already met.

theme: Chronos query is append-only read — never mutates tablets
insight: Chronos's contract is READ-ONLY. wing_chronos_query reads tablet files, aggregates stats, and returns them — it never writes to any tablet. This is the Chronicler-sovereignty principle: Chroniclers own their tablets; Chronos borrows the view. Mutation-free query is foundational for governance audit: historical state is immutable once written by write_chronicler. Future Chronos implementations (K517+) may add indexed caching, but must never backfill or rewrite Chronicler tablet entries.

theme: Bureau subscription is pull (poll) not push (stream)
insight: bureau_subscribe is implemented as pull-mode (read recent chunks filtered by risk_filter). True push-mode streaming (websocket, SSE) requires infrastructure not yet in the Helm PWA architecture. The pull-mode implementation is sufficient for K515 because the usage pattern is: Bishop polls the Bureau at step boundaries, not continuously. Future K-sessions (post-K519 NAF federation) may add push-mode when cross-agent real-time risk signaling becomes load-bearing.

theme: 7 risk-pattern Augurs cover the canonical failure modes
insight: The 7 Embedded-Correspondent Augurs (vendor-secret-rotation, force-push, schema-destruction, filesystem-wipe, permission-grant, api-spend-spike, toolsmith-missing-at-ratification) cover the full canonical failure-mode taxonomy from BRIDLE v10.5 + B126 canon files. The library is open for extension (new Augurs are just new entries in RISK_PATTERN_AUGURS list), but these 7 are the founding members because they each have a B126-ratified canonical source in the memory files.

theme: Performance profile: 10k-entry tablets query in <50ms
insight: Both wing_chronos_query (C.8: 49.7ms) and query_bureau (C.16: 24.4ms) query 10,000-entry tablets in under 50ms — well under the 200ms p95 budget. This is because both use simple line-by-line JSONL reading without an index; at this scale, Python's I/O is fast enough. Future indexing (B-tree per augur_id, timestamp index) should only be added when tablet sizes regularly exceed 100k entries — don't optimize prematurely.

theme: Fail-safe principle applies to all new K515 modules
insight: bureau.py and chronicler.py both follow the same fail-safe principle as the Wing engine: any write error is silently swallowed (try/except pass). This means a disk-full condition, permissions error, or corrupted tablet can never break Knight's actual work. The observation system MUST NEVER interfere with the observed system. The cost of this trade-off: some reasoning chunks or Augur evaluations may not be logged. This is acceptable; governance is best-effort in failure conditions.

theme: MCP tool naming convention: flat names, no prefix
insight: The K515 MCP tools are named chronos_query, correspondent_log, bureau_subscribe, bureau_query — NOT mcp__librarian__chronos_query as the K515 prompt specified. The tool names in server.ts must match what callers invoke via the MCP protocol. The mcp__librarian__ prefix is a namespaced reference convention used in architecture docs; the actual tool registration uses the bare name. Future A&A/K-prompts should spec tool names in both forms to avoid confusion.

theme: ChronosBureauPanel uses two-tab architecture (Chronos tab + Bureau tab)
insight: Separating Chronos (component-state, Augur fire rates) and Bureau (reasoning-state, advisory feed) into tabs within one component maintains architectural symmetry in the UI. Single component = single import in App.tsx; two tabs = independent data fetching. This mirrors the Python module structure (chronicler.py and bureau.py are separate modules in the same package). Future K-sessions can add a third tab (Federation) for cross-NAF aggregate views.

theme: WORKSPACE_ROOT constant already existed in server.ts at line 882
insight: The librarian-mcp/src/server.ts had WORKSPACE_ROOT = resolve(__dirname, '..', '..') already defined at line 882 for a different tool. Adding a new constant with the same name caused a TypeScript 'Cannot redeclare block-scoped variable' error. Pattern for future additions to server.ts: search for existing constants before declaring new ones. The fix was to remove the duplicate declaration and reuse the existing constant.

theme: Component-state Augurs vs Reasoning-state Augurs — scoping distinction
insight: Wing Augurs (K514, in discipline_wing/engine.py) watch TOOL CALLS: they trigger on Write/Edit events with path and content matching. Embedded-Correspondent Augurs (K515, in discipline_wing/bureau.py) watch REASONING CHUNKS: they trigger on text content without file-path scoping. This is intentional: reasoning Augurs can't know which file will be written yet (the reasoning precedes the tool call). The scoping distinction means the two Augur classes are complementary, not overlapping. Wing Augurs catch 'you are writing forbidden content'; EC Augurs catch 'you are reasoning toward a forbidden action'.

theme: Tablet-path conventions across Chroniclers and Correspondents
insight: Chronicler tablets: ~/.claude/state/chroniclers/augur_<id>.jsonl. Correspondent tablets: ~/.claude/state/correspondents/agent_<agent>_session_<session>.jsonl. Both use ~/.claude/state/ as root (personal-scope, never committed to git). Both are append-only JSONL. The naming convention makes tablets self-describing: augur_ prefix → Augur id; agent_ prefix → agent name + session pair. Do NOT change these paths post-K515 without migrating existing tablet data.

theme: gitignore carve-out covers tests_k515.py automatically
insight: The K514 gitignore carve-out added explicit !discipline_wing/*.py rules. This automatically includes tests_k515.py (added in K515) without any further gitignore changes. The carve-out was designed to be session-proof: any future .py file added to discipline_wing/ is tracked automatically. Future K-sessions that add .py files to discipline_wing/ do NOT need to update .gitignore.

theme: Bureau sovereignty: reasoning chunks stay per-agent, cross-agent only via Bureau query
insight: Correspondent tablets are personal-scope (per-agent per-session). Cross-agent visibility only occurs via bureau_subscribe (explicitly pull-based with watching_agent parameter) or bureau_query. No agent can read another agent's tablet directly — only through the Bureau's mediated interface. This preserves the Sovereignty Preservation principle from K515 constraints. Federation across NAFs (K519+) MUST follow the same mediation pattern: never raw tablet sharing, always Bureau-mediated.

theme: C.14b bonus check proves anti-pattern clearance is bidirectional
insight: The K515 prompt specified 16 checks; the test suite ran 17 (C.14b added as a bonus). C.14b proves that a ratification chunk WITH a TS-id citation correctly clears Augur-Toolsmith-Missing (triggered=False). This bidirectional test (C.14: no citation → fires; C.14b: citation present → doesn't fire) is the minimum bar for any Augur with an anti-pattern guard. Future Augurs with anti-patterns should always include both directions in the test suite.

theme: Helm PWA Observers nav item — third functional view after Wing and Rules
insight: K515 adds a third discipline-plane view to the Helm PWA sidebar: Wing (Augur telemetry, K514), Observers (Chronos+Bureau twin-observer data, K515), Rules (Discipline Rule Editor, K513). The nav icon is a telescope emoji. The view uses ChronosBureauPanel with two internal tabs. Future views: Dragonriders (K516), TimeWave (K517). The sidebar is approaching its capacity; K518+ may need a drawer or collapsible sections.

theme: INNOVATION_THRESH_2306 promoted to shipped status — Prov 14/15 decision pending
insight: INNOVATION_THRESH_2306_B126_EMBEDDED_CORRESPONDENT_BUREAU.md was updated with K515 reduction-to-practice anchor and all verification evidence. The thresh is now shipping, not queued. The Prov 14 amendment vs Prov 15 batch filing decision is Founder-call. Priority date is 2026-04-26 (filing window applies if Prov 14 amendment is filed today). The K512.5 regression proof (C.12) is the load-bearing empirical anchor for patent claim 1(a) and 1(d) — the failure-mode is proven prevented in production.
